results.name: iShares Bitcoin ETF
keyThemes: ETF inflow rebound after extended outflow streak
BlackRock IBIT institutional dominance in trading volume
Long-dated options and LEAPS interest growing
Bitcoin macro bottoming signals driving accumulation
results.topBullish:
Crypto ETF Inflows Rebound

On July 10, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $90.4M in net inflows, led by BlackRock's IBIT with $86.8M. Spot Ethereum ETFs also added $18.4M. The rebound marked Bitcoin ETFs' first weekly net inflow since May.

#BTC #BitcoinETF #EthereumETF #IBIT
results.topBearish: $221M single-day inflow into Bitcoin ETFs ended a 10-day, $2.73B outflow run. FBTC led; $IBIT saw outflows. Tactical re-entry by non-BlackRock funds is a structurally different signal than passive flows. via CoinDesk
results.summary: IBIT sentiment is meaningfully bullish, driven by a confirmed reversal in ETF inflows after a prolonged 10-day, $2.73B outflow period, with BlackRock reclaiming flow leadership at $86.8M on July 10 and dominating spot BTC ETF trading volume at $1.11B in a single session. Retail and institutional participants are initiating or expressing interest in long-dated calls and LEAPS, citing historical Bitcoin bottoming patterns and macro momentum, though a cautionary note persists that stripping IBIT from the broader ETF complex still reveals residual weakness.

results.topBullish: Posting this as a counter to the bearish sentiment creeping in on the AI Data Centre stocks lately. All these stocks have had phenomenal runs in the last 3 years, except $HIVE & $KEEL which I believe is going to change this year.
results.topBearish: $KEEL still stuck in a bearish trend, no real support carved out yet. Patience matters more than action here. The level everyone is watching is $5.00, a candle body close above that psychological line is what would flip the picture bullish. Until then it's a watch, not a chase.
results.summary: Sentiment on $KEEL is cautiously bearish, with the dominant organic narrative centering on a confirmed downtrend and no established support, with traders broadly treating $5.00 as the critical inflection point before any bullish thesis can be validated. The signal is significantly diluted by a high volume of spam and promotional posts, limiting the quality of actionable insight, though a small cohort of bulls cite AI infrastructure tailwinds and Elliott Wave projections toward $10 as longer-term upside cases.
